title: A2 Final Challenge
description: Complete all 20 questions to unlock B1 level

points.title: A2 Mastery Test
points.description: Prove your mastery of A2 phrasal verbs

questions.question: Which phrasal verb means 'to stop sleeping'?
questions.feedback: Correct! 'Wake up' means to stop sleeping.

questions.sentence: Can you ______ ______ the lights? I can't see anything.
questions.feedback: 'Turn on' means to activate a device.

questions.sentence: We got ______ the taxi at the airport.
questions.feedback: 'Get out of' is used for leaving small vehicles.
Separable verbs split with pronouns: 'Put them away'.

questions.sentence: The children ______ ______ (left the house) to play in the garden.
questions.feedback: 'Went out' (past of 'go out') means to leave a place.

questions.question: Which sentence uses a pronoun correctly with a separable phrasal verb?
questions.feedback: Pronouns must go between the verb and particle: 'Turn it off'.
Perfect: 'He woke up very early'.

questions.context: A: Someone's knocking at the door. B: ______ ______! It's open!
questions.feedback: 'Come in' is a friendly way to invite someone to enter.

questions.sentence: I usually ______ ______ (leave bed) at 7, but today I slept in.
questions.feedback: 'Get up' means to leave your bed.

questions.sentence: Please put your coat ______. It's on the floor.
questions.feedback: 'Put away' means to put something in its proper place.

questions.question: 'Get in' is used with which type of transport?
questions.feedback: 'Get in' for small vehicles (car, taxi). 'Get on' for large ones (bus, train, plane).

questions.sentence: Don't forget to ______ ______ the computer before you leave.
questions.feedback: 'Turn off' means to make a device stop working.

questions.context: A: I dropped my pen. B: I'll ______ it ______ for you.
questions.feedback: With a pronoun, the object goes between verb and particle: 'pick it up'.
'Let's have a break' - time to rest!

questions.question: What does 'come back' mean?
questions.feedback: 'Come back' means to return to a place.

questions.sentence: I feel dizzy. I need to ______ ______ (rest horizontally).
questions.feedback: 'Lie down' means to put yourself in a horizontal position to rest.

questions.sentence: What time did you come ______ from school?
questions.feedback: 'Come back' means to return.
Separable verbs must split with pronouns: 'Turn it on'.

questions.sentence: She ______ ______ (entered) the room and said hello to everyone.
questions.feedback: 'Came in' (past of 'come in') means to enter a room.

questions.question: Which of these phrasal verbs is INSEPARABLE?
questions.feedback: 'Get up' is inseparable and intransitive - it doesn't take an object.
